title: News Feed
prompt: Design a social media news feed system like Facebook's or Twitter's timeline. Users should see a personalized feed of posts from people they follow, ranked by relevance and recency.

functional: Users can create posts (text, images, links)
Users see a personalized feed of posts from followed users
Feed is ranked by a combination of recency and relevance
nonFunctional: New posts appear in followers' feeds within 5 seconds
Handle celebrity users with millions of followers

keyQuestions: What types of content? Text only or multimedia?
How should posts be ranked? Pure chronological or relevance?
How many users does a typical user follow?
Do we need to handle celebrity users (millions of followers)?
What is the acceptable latency for feed generation?
Do we need real-time updates or is pull-to-refresh acceptable?

keyCalculations: Feed cache: 200M DAU * 500 posts cache = 100B cached entries
Storage: 10B posts * 1KB avg = 10TB/year text, much more with media

expectedEntities: posts: id, author_id, content, media_urls[], created_at
feed_cache: user_id, post_id, score, created_at (materialized feed)

expectedComponents: Feed Cache (per-user materialized feed in Redis)

suggestedTopics: Fan-out on write vs fan-out on read (hybrid approach for celebrities)
Feed ranking algorithm and real-time re-ranking
Cache design: pre-computed feed lists in Redis sorted sets
Celebrity user problem: threshold-based hybrid fan-out

anchors.1: Skipped requirements and started drawing boxes
anchors.2: Asked about basic features but missed celebrity problem or ranking
anchors.3: Covered functional requirements, ranking, scale, and celebrity edge case
anchors.4: Identified fan-out challenge upfront, asked about consistency requirements, and quantified scale

checklist: Separated post ingestion from feed generation

anchors.1: Direct database query for feed on each request, no caching
anchors.2: Basic architecture with post store and simple query-based feed
anchors.3: Pre-computed feed cache, fan-out mechanism, separate ranking service
anchors.4: Hybrid fan-out architecture with justified component boundaries and failure isolation

checklist: Feed cache structure and update mechanism
Handling post updates/deletes in cached feeds

anchors.1: No concrete implementation details for any component
anchors.2: Described fan-out at surface level without celebrity handling
anchors.3: Detailed hybrid fan-out strategy, concrete cache design, ranking approach
anchors.4: Quantitative analysis of fan-out costs, cache hit ratios, ranking feature engineering

checklist: Fan-out on write vs read (latency vs storage)
Chronological vs ranked feed (freshness vs engagement)
Consistency vs availability for feed updates
Cache memory cost vs query latency

anchors.1: Could not articulate any design trade-offs
anchors.2: Mentioned fan-out trade-off without quantitative reasoning
anchors.3: Analyzed 2-3 trade-offs with clear justification for chosen approach
anchors.4: Systematic analysis with numbers (e.g., fan-out cost per celebrity post) and justified hybrid approaches

commonMistakes: Pure fan-out on write without handling celebrity users (10M follower fan-out on every post)
Not pre-computing feeds (querying social graph + posts on every feed request)
Ignoring feed consistency when posts are deleted or edited
Not considering ranking and just showing reverse chronological
Using a single relational database for the social graph at scale
Not addressing cache invalidation when users unfollow
